Adventure Section PARANOIA 
Wi Somebody's Got to Take the 
Fall 
Grund-G, an HPD & MC guard unit non: com with valuable contacts in the Armed Forces, was picked to head the mission. Because af his dubious political reliability, it was decided that Grund-G be kept in the dark about the real objectives of the mission; in- stead, he was told that this is jus alittle survey mission to update files on the region; if the 
paary can also figure out what happened tothe. missing mission group and salvage their equip- ‘ment, all the better. ‘Grund, a veteran Troubleshooter and sur vivor of numerous inter-service power plays, suspects that all is not kosher with the mis- sion; he has a few surprises of his own up his, sleeve, 
Adventure Summary 
I Episode One: The Briefing The Troubleshooters, eagerly looking for- ward to serving ther friend The Computer and, Alpha Complex, attempt to discover the loca tion of their briefing room, The survivors (if Any) and the clone replacements of the others meet their briefing officers, receive additional Instructions, meet Grund-G, their new team, leader, and get to ask any questions they may have about the mission. A spirited firefight, probably ensues, as loyal Troubleshooters, foast any traitors who ask for information not available at their security clearance. 
WW Episode Two: Outfitting at PLC 
Grund:G ushers the survivors (if any) and clone replacements off to PLC Outfitting t0 receive mission equipment, On the way, Grund-G reinforces his authority over and con- tempt for the Troubleshooters and picks a sec- ‘ond in command. While picking up three In- frared drones assigned to the mission, Grund= slips away fora few minutes to contact his, secret society. Prudent Troubleshooters do the 
‘ALPLC, the mission group receives lots of interesting and useful equipment in perfect, ‘operating condition, Grund encourages one ‘of the PCs to sign for this equipment. Grund- G divides the equipment up, and the survivors proceed to R&D where they will volunteer to, test experimental equipment. 
I Episode Thre: isit to 
Research and Design ‘After a fun ride in an experimental autoshut- tle, the mission group reaches PDQ Sector R&D labs. Eager to serve The Computer, yain large credit bonuses, and avoid reassignment to freefall impact simulation testing, some Troubleshooters agree to test experimental equipment on their mission. Boom. Like it or ‘not, they all agree to test the new Sensurround Team Communications System and Loyalty Officer Control Module. Screceece-zhippp- wheez-crackle-pop...o0m. 
Fach Paranoia gamemaster has his own methods for keeping players in line. 
Enjoying another ride in the experimental autoshuttle the mission group is introduced to the Multi-Terrain Vehicle (MTV), and Lucky, the MTV's autopilot. The team, the Infrareds, and all the equipment is loaded onto the MTV; the huge, barred doors rumble open; and MTV trundles into the Outdoors. 
I Episode Four: Voyage to See What's on the Bottom ‘The team experiences the thrill of undersea travel in an old leaky subbot with an autopilot suffering from intermittent senile dementia, 
I Episode Five: 
To the Shores of Tripoli 
MTV reaches land. Grund-G, deciding that the mission is a bust, runs off into the bush and snipes at the Troubleshooters. The Troubleshooters choose a new mission leader. Survivors attempt to continue the mission, 
I Episode Six: Saturday Night Fever 
Lucky the autopilot spazzes out. The Troubleshooters chase the MTV until it gets stuck in a tree. Having no other way to get home, they ty 0 fx ft 
I Episode Seven: 
Tenting Tonight on the Old Tent Ground 
‘The Troubleshooters spend the night Out- 
doors. Nothing much happens except that Grund. sneaks up and attempts to kil all of them and steal the MTV. He probably doesn’t succeed, 
IW ipisodes Eight, Nine, and Ten: More Fun 
Screaming hordes of spear-waving savages descend onthe Troubleshooters, The Touble- shooters laser them into submission, bribe them to leave them alone, convice thery that they are gods, or just get killed and eaten ‘Questioning the natives reveals that there are new gods, "walking mountains,” wander: ing the countryside; the natives suggest that the Troubleshooters go and visit the gods and 
leave them alone, The walking mountains put 
ian appearance. The mountains are revealed as giant mutant cockroaches searching the country looking for 
culture, They ask the Troubleshooters to bring ‘them Renoir and Proust. Troubleshooters who 
claim never to have heard of any such thing. 
are killed a little. 
Surviving Troubleshooters probably agree to go back to Alpha Complex and get some: 
Renoir and Proust. They return to Alpha Com- 
plex and are mostly killed during the debrief- 
ing. Maybe a couple earn big commendations. Several” make conflicting accusations of ‘treason against other Troubleshooters and are 
killed. Others end up owing millions of credits 
for los/damaged mission equipment and are demoted and reassigned to plankton farm- 
ing/low-yield biological substance testing 
combines until their debts are paid off. 
The End,

Troubleshooters clone dies in Aipha Com- plex, HPD & MC officials simply send for the
